examples.title: Compute Binomial Coefficient C(n,k)
examples.description: Calculate binomial coefficient using recursive formula based on Pascal's Identity
examples.code:
FUNCTION Binomial(n, k):
  IF k == 0 OR k == n THEN
    RETURN 1
  ELSE IF k > n THEN
    RETURN 0
  ELSE
    RETURN Binomial(n-1, k-1) + Binomial(n-1, k)
  ENDIF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Uses Pascal's Identity recursively: C(n,k) = C(n-1,k-1) + C(n-1,k). Base cases: C(n,0) = C(n,n) = 1.
examples.timeComplexity: O(2^n) in worst case (recursive tree depth)

examples.title: Binomial Coefficient using Dynamic Programming
examples.description: Efficient O(n×k) computation using Pascal's Triangle building
examples.code:
FUNCTION BinomialDP(n, k):
  // Create Pascal's triangle row by row
  IF k > n-k THEN
    k = n-k  // Use symmetry property
  ENDIF
  
  CREATE array dp[0..k] initialized to 0
  dp[0] = 1
  
  FOR i FROM 1 TO n DO
    FOR j FROM min(i, k) DOWN TO 1 DO
      dp[j] = dp[j] + dp[j-1]
    ENDFOR
  ENDFOR
  
  RETURN dp[k]
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Builds Pascal's Triangle row by row. Uses symmetry to minimize k. dp[j] represents C(i,j) after processing row i.

examples.title: Direct Formula Implementation
examples.description: Compute C(n,k) using factorial formula with optimization
examples.code:
import math
from functools import lru_cache

# Direct formula method
def binomial_direct(n, k):
    """Using factorial formula with overflow handling"""
    if k < 0 or k > n:
        return 0
    if k == 0 or k == n:
        return 1
    # Use symmetry
    k = min(k, n - k)
    result = 1
    for i in range(1, k + 1):
        result = result * (n - k + i) // i
    return result

# Test
print(f"C(5,2) = {binomial_direct(5,2)}")  # 10
print(f"C(10,5) = {binomial_direct(10,5)}")  # 252
print(f"C(20,10) = {binomial_direct(20,10)}")  # 184756
examples.explanation: Multiplicative formula: C(n,k) = ∏(n-k+i)/i for i=1 to k. Avoids large factorials by computing product incrementally and dividing at each step. Uses symmetry to minimize iterations.

examples.title: Generate Pascal's Triangle
examples.description: Build complete Pascal's Triangle up to n rows
examples.explanation: Builds Pascal's Triangle row by row. Each entry is sum of two entries above it from previous row. First and last entries of each row are 1.
